mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.50% and remains there for at least 2 consecutive months, the zero-lower-bound constraint becomes binding. This signals exhausted conventional monetary policy and triggers equity risk-off behavior as investors anticipate policy impotence during future downturns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02 in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more signals deteriorating labor market conditions and rising recession risk. This triggers a forward-looking equity sell-off as investors reprice earnings expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 1% month-over-month while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600 thousand units annualized. This joint signal indicates weakening consumer demand and housing investment, the two largest components of GDP. The mechanism predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth < 1% AND HOUST < 600k units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate remains above 9.0%. This signals a supply-side contraction coinciding with labor slack, indicating stagflationary pressure. Equities decline as profit margins compress under rising input costs and weak demand, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE > 9.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the joint condition shows no incremental predictive power over UNRATE alone (Rank IC difference < 0.015).

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates to > 200k jobs added month-over-month for two consecutive months while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines. This dual signal indicates a robust labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > 200k for 2 consecutive months AND UNRATE declines
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect adds no incremental predictive power (Rank IC difference < 0.015).

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real interest rate environment. This signals monetary accommodation during inflationary pressure, which historically precedes equity market volatility and mean reversion as the Fed eventually tightens. Mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: negative real rates with rising inflation regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the real rate signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond nominal FEDFUNDS (Rank IC difference < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) trend upward for three consecutive months while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive and above 2% annualized. This always-on mechanism captures the secular relationship between housing investment and equity valuations: sustained housing strength signals durable demand and supports construction employment and materials demand, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of periods when both HOUST and GDPC1 are in their respective upper terciles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) grows month-over-month by > 0.5% while simultaneously nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) increases by > 150k jobs. This always-on mechanism captures synchronized supply-side and labor-side expansion, indicating robust economic momentum that supports equity valuations through higher corporate output and employment. Mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of periods when both INDPRO MoM > 0.5% and PAYEMS MoM > 150k.
